name: Polenta and Sausages

ingredients.item: can San Marzano tomatoes plus 1 can water

method: In a large frying pan on medium heat, add 2 T olive oil, brown sausage links. Add onion and saute until tender. Add tomatoes and break up the larger bits, garlic, basil, oregano, pepper, 1 tsp salt, and water. Cover with a lid, simmer for 3 hours on low heat stirring occasionally. Add water if needed. Before the sausages are ready, prepare the polenta. I use quick polenta and it takes about 5 minutes. In a medium saucepan on high, add 3 cups of water and 1 t salt and bring it to a boil as you would when making rice. Lower heat to medium. Add one cup of polenta and butter. Stir slowly until the polenta thickens, if it becomes too thick, add water. The consistency of the polenta is similiar to very thick cream of wheat cereal. When polenta is very thick remove from heat. It will become harder and harder to stir. Scoop onto plates and pour sauce over the top Place sausages on the side or on top and pour more sauce over sausage. Sprinkle parmesan or pecorino on top, the cheese isn't shown here because I wanted to show you the sausages, sauce, and polenta clearly so you can see how thick the polenta is. Serving suggestion: fresh bread and salad. Serves 2-3. Olé
